v.CNV: 耶稣又进了会堂，在那里有一个人，他的一只手枯干了。
v.ESV: Again he entered the synagogue, and a man was there with a withered hand.
v.GAE: 예수께서 다시 회당에 들어가시니 한쪽 손 마른 사람이 거기 있는지라
v.KJV: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.
v.NASB: He entered again into a synagogue; and a man was there whose hand was withered.
v.NIV: Another time Jesus went into the synagogue, and a man with a shriveled hand was there.
v.NRSV: Again he entered the synagogue, and a man was there who had a withered hand.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 다시 회당에 들어가셨다. 그런데 거기에 한쪽 손이 오그라든 사람이 있었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 다시 회당으로 들어가셨는데 그곳에 한쪽 손이 오그라든 사람이 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 众人窥探他会不会在安息日医治那个人，好去控告他。
v.ESV: And they watched Jesus, to see whether he would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse him.
v.GAE: 사람들이 예수를 고발하려 하여 안식일에 그 사람을 고치시는가 주시하고 있거늘
v.KJV: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
v.NASB: They were watching Him to see if He would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse Him.
v.NIV: Some of them were looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, so they watched him closely to see if he would heal him on the Sabbath.
v.NRSV: They watched him to see whether he would cure him on the sabbath, so that they might accuse him.
v.SAENEW: 사람들은 예수를 고발하려고, 예수가 안식일에 그 사람을 고쳐 주시는지를 보려고, 예수를 지켜보고 있었다.
v.WLB: 몇몇 사람들이 혹시 예수께서 안식일에 그 사람을 고치지나 않을까 하며 예수를 고소할 구실을 찾으려고 가까이에서 지켜보고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣对那一只手枯干了的人说：“起来，站在当中！”
v.ESV: And he said to the man with the withered hand, “Come here.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 손 마른 사람에게 이르시되 한 가운데에 일어서라 하시고
v.KJV: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
v.NASB: He said to the man with the withered hand, 'Get up and come forward!'
v.NIV: Jesus said to the man with the shriveled hand, “Stand up in front of everyone.”
v.NRSV: And he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Come forward.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 손이 오그라든 사람에게 말씀하셨다. "일어나서 가운데로 나오너라."
v.WLB: 예수께서 손이 오그라든 사람에게 말씀하셨습니다. “일어나 앞으로 나오너라.”

v.CNV: 又对他们说：“在安息日哪一样是可以作的呢：作好事还是坏事？救命还是害命？”他们一声不响。
v.ESV: And he said to them, “Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do harm, to save life or to kill?” But they were silent.
v.GAE: 그들에게 이르시되 안식일에 선을 행하는 것과 악을 행하는 것, 생명을 구하는 것과 죽이는 것, 어느 것이 옳으냐 하시니 그들이 잠잠하거늘
v.KJV: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.
v.NASB: And He said to them, 'Is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the Sabbath, to save a life or to kill?' But they kept silent.
v.NIV: Then Jesus asked them, “Which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill?” But they remained silent.
v.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the sabbath, to save life or to kill?” But they were silent.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "안식일에 선한 일을 하는 것이 옳으냐? 악한 일을 하는 것이 옳으냐? 목숨을 구하는 것이 옳으냐? 죽이는 것이 옳으냐?" 그들은 잠잠하였다.
v.WLB: 그리고 예수께서 그들에게 물으셨습니다. “안식일에 무슨 일을 하는 것이 옳겠느냐? 선한 일이냐, 악한 일이냐? 생명을 구하는 것이냐, 죽이는 것이냐?” 그러자 그들은 말없이 잠자코 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣怒目环视他们，因他们的心刚硬而难过，就对那人说：“伸出手来！”他把手一伸，手就复原了。
v.ESV: And he looked around at them with anger, grieved at their hardness of heart, and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
v.GAE: 그들의 마음이 완악함을 탄식하사 노하심으로 그들을 둘러 보시고 그 사람에게 이르시되 네 손을 내밀라 하시니 내밀매 그 손이 회복되었더라
v.KJV: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
v.NASB: After looking around at them with anger, grieved at their hardness of heart, He said to the man, 'Stretch out your hand.' And he stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
v.NIV: He looked around at them in anger and, deeply distressed at their stubborn hearts, said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was completely restored.
v.NRSV: He looked around at them with anger; he was grieved at their hardness of heart and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 노하셔서, 그들을 둘러보시고, 그들의 마음이 굳어진 것을 탄식하시면서, 손이 오그라든 사람에게 말씀하셨다. "손을 내밀어라." 그 사람이 손을 내미니, 그의 손이 회복되었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 노하셔서 그들을 둘러보시고 그들의 마음이 완악한 것을 마음 깊이 슬퍼하시며 그 사람에게 말씀하셨습니다. “손을 펴 보아라.” 그가 손을 쭉 내밀자 그 손이 완전하게 회복됐습니다.

v.CNV: 法利赛人出来，立刻和希律党人商量怎样对付耶稣，好除掉他。
v.ESV: The Pharisees went out and immediately held counsel with the Herodians against him, how to destroy him.
v.GAE: 바리새인들이 나가서 곧 헤롯당과 함께 어떻게 하여 예수를 죽일까 의논하니라
v.KJV: And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
v.NASB: The Pharisees went out and immediately began conspiring with the Herodians against Him, as to how they might destroy Him.
v.NIV: Then the Pharisees went out and began to plot with the Herodians how they might kill Jesus.
v.NRSV: The Pharisees went out and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how to destroy him.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 바리새파 사람들은 바깥으로 나가서, 곧바로 헤롯 당원들과 함께 예수를 없앨 모의를 하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 바리새파 사람들은 그 길로 나가 헤롯 당원들과 함께 어떻게 하면 예수를 죽일까 음모를 꾸미기 시작했습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣和门徒退到海边去，一大群从加利利来的人跟着他；
v.ESV: Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the sea, and a great crowd followed, from Galilee and Judea
v.GAE: 예수께서 제자들과 함께 바다로 물러가시니 갈릴리에서 큰 무리가 따르며
v.KJV: But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judaea,
v.NASB: Jesus withdrew to the sea with His disciples; and a great multitude from Galilee followed; and also from Judea,
v.NIV: Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the lake, and a large crowd from Galilee followed.
v.NRSV: Jesus departed with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed him;
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 제자들과 함께 바닷가로 물러가시니, 갈릴리에서 많은 사람이 따라왔다. 또한 유대와
v.WLB: 예수께서 제자들을 데리고 호숫가로 물러가시자 갈릴리에서 많은 사람들이 예수를 따라왔습니다.

v.CNV: 还有许多人听见他所作的一切事，就从犹太、耶路撒冷、以土迈、约旦河外和推罗、西顿一带地方来到他跟前。
v.ESV: and Jerusalem and Idumea and from beyond the Jordan and from around Tyre and Sidon. When the great crowd heard all that he was doing, they came to him.
v.GAE: 유대와 예루살렘과 이두매와 요단 강 건너편과 또 두로와 시돈 근처에서 많은 무리가 그가 하신 큰 일을 듣고 나아오는지라
v.KJV: And from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.
v.NASB: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumea, and beyond the Jordan, and the vicinity of Tyre and Sidon, a great number of people heard of all that He was doing and came to Him.
v.NIV: When they heard about all he was doing, many people came to him from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, and the regions across the Jordan and around Tyre and Sidon.
v.NRSV: hearing all that he was doing, they came to him in great numbers from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the region around Tyre and Sidon.
v.SAENEW: 예루살렘과 이두매와 요단 강 건너편과 그리고 두로와 시돈 근처에서도, 많은 사람이 그가 하신 모든 일을 소문으로 듣고, 그에게로 몰려왔다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 행하신 일을 다 듣고 유대, 예루살렘, 이두매, 요단 강 건너편, 두로와 시돈 지방에서 많은 사람들이 몰려왔습니다.

v.CNV: 因为人多，耶稣就吩咐门徒为他预备一只小船，免得众人拥挤他。
v.ESV: And he told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, lest they crush him,
v.GAE: 예수께서 무리가 에워싸 미는 것을 피하기 위하여 작은 배를 대기하도록 제자들에게 명하셨으니
v.KJV: And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
v.NASB: And He told His disciples that a boat should stand ready for Him because of the crowd, so that they would not crowd Him;
v.NIV: Because of the crowd he told his disciples to have a small boat ready for him, to keep the people from crowding him.
v.NRSV: He told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, so that they would not crush him;
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 무리가 자기에게 밀려드는 혼잡을 피하시려고, 제자들에게 분부하여 작은 배 한 척을 마련하게 하셨다.
v.WLB: 사람들이 너무 많아서 예수께서는 제자들에게 작은 배 하나를 마련하라고 말씀하셨습니다. 무리가 자기에게 몰려드는 것을 막으려는 것이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他医好了许多人，所以凡有病的都挤过来要摸他。
v.ESV: for he had healed many, so that all who had diseases pressed around him to touch him.
v.GAE: 이는 많은 사람을 고치셨으므로 병으로 고생하는 자들이 예수를 만지고자 하여 몰려왔음이더라
v.KJV: For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
v.NASB: for He had healed many, with the result that all those who had afflictions pressed around Him in order to touch Him.
v.NIV: For he had healed many, so that those with diseases were pushing forward to touch him.
v.NRSV: for he had cured many, so that all who had diseases pressed upon him to touch him.
v.SAENEW: 그가 많은 사람을 고쳐 주셨으므로, 온갖 병으로 고통받는 사람들이, 누구나 그에게 손을 대려고 밀려들었기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 전에 많은 사람들을 고쳐 주셨기 때문에 온갖 병에 걸린 사람들이 예수를 만지기 위해 밀려들었던 것입니다.

v.CNV: 污灵每次看到他，就仆倒在他面前，大声喊叫说：“你是　神的儿子！”
v.ESV: And whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and cried out, “You are the Son of God.”
v.GAE: 더러운 귀신들도 어느 때든지 예수를 보면 그 앞에 엎드려 부르짖어 이르되 당신은 하나님의 아들이니이다 하니
v.KJV: And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
v.NASB: Whenever the unclean spirits saw Him, they would fall down before Him and shout, 'You are the Son of God!'
v.NIV: Whenever the impure spirits saw him, they fell down before him and cried out, “You are the Son of God.”
v.NRSV: Whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and shouted, “You are the Son of God!”
v.SAENEW: 또 악한 귀신들은 예수를 보기만 하면, 그 앞에 엎드려서 외쳤다. "당신은 하나님의 아들입니다."
v.WLB: 더러운 귀신들은 예수를 보기만 하면 그 앞에 엎드러져 “당신은 하나님의 아들이십니다”라고 소리쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣再三严厉地吩咐他们，不要把他的身分张扬出去。
v.ESV: And he strictly ordered them not to make him known.
v.GAE: 예수께서 자기를 나타내지 말라고 많이 경고하시니라
v.KJV: And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.
v.NASB: And He earnestly warned them not to tell who He was.
v.NIV: But he gave them strict orders not to tell others about him.
v.NRSV: But he sternly ordered them not to make him known.
v.SAENEW: 그러면 예수께서는 "나를 세상에 드러내지 말아라" 하고, 그들을 엄하게 꾸짖으셨다.
v.WLB: 그러나 예수께서는 자기가 누구인지 말하지 말라고 엄하게 꾸짖으셨습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣上了山，呼召自己所要的人，他们就来了。
v.ESV: And he went up on the mountain and called to him those whom he desired, and they came to him.
v.GAE: 또 산에 오르사 자기가 원하는 자들을 부르시니 나아온지라
v.KJV: And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.
v.NASB: And He went up on the mountain and summoned those whom He Himself wanted, and they came to Him.
v.NIV: Jesus went up on a mountainside and called to him those he wanted, and they came to him.
v.NRSV: He went up the mountain and called to him those whom he wanted, and they came to him.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 산에 올라가셔서, 원하시는 사람들을 부르시니, 그들이 예수께로 나아왔다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 산으로 올라가셔서 원하는 사람들을 불러 모으셨습니다. 그러자 그들이 예수께로 나아왔습니다.

v.CNV: 他选立了十二个人，称他们为使徒（有些抄本无“称他们为使徒”一句），要他们跟自己常在一起，好差遣他们去传道，
v.ESV: And he appointed twelve (whom he also named apostles) so that they might be with him and he might send them out to preach
v.GAE: 이에 열둘을 세우셨으니 이는 자기와 함께 있게 하시고 또 보내사 전도도 하며
v.KJV: And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,
v.NASB: And He appointed twelve, so that they would be with Him and that He could send them out to preach,
v.NIV: He appointed twelve that they might be with him and that he might send them out to preach
v.NRSV: And he appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, to be with him, and to be sent out to proclaim the message,
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 열둘을 세우시고 [그들을 또한 사도라고 이름하셨다.] 이것은, 예수께서 그들을 자기와 함께 있게 하시고, 또 그들을 내보내어서 말씀을 전파하게 하시며,
v.WLB: 예수께서는 12명을 따로 뽑아 (이들을 사도라 부르시고) 자기와 함께 있게 하셨습니다. 그리고 그들을 내보내셔서 전도도 하게 하시며

v.CNV: 又有权柄赶鬼。
v.ESV: and have authority to cast out demons.
v.GAE: 귀신을 내쫓는 권능도 가지게 하려 하심이러라
v.KJV: And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:
v.NASB: and to have authority to cast out the demons.
v.NIV: and to have authority to drive out demons.
v.NRSV: and to have authority to cast out demons.
v.SAENEW: 귀신을 쫓아내는 권능을 가지게 하시려는 것이었다.
v.WLB: 그들에게 귀신을 쫓는 권세도 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他选立的十二个人是：西门（耶稣给他起名叫彼得），
v.ESV: He appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
v.GAE: 이 열둘을 세우셨으니 시몬에게는 베드로란 이름을 더하셨고
v.KJV: And Simon he surnamed Peter;
v.NASB: And He appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom He gave the name Peter),
v.NIV: These are the twelve he appointed: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter),
v.NRSV: So he appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
v.SAENEW: [예수께서 열둘을 임명하셨는데,] 그들은, 베드로라는 이름을 덧붙여 주신 시몬과,
v.WLB: 예수께서 세우신 12사람들은 베드로라 이름 지어 준 시몬,

v.CNV: 西庇太的儿子雅各，和雅各的弟弟约翰（耶稣给他们二人起名叫半尼其，就是“雷子”的意思），
v.ESV: James the son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder);
v.GAE: 또 세베대의 아들 야고보와 야고보의 형제 요한이니 이 둘에게는 보아너게 곧 우레의 아들이란 이름을 더하셨으며
v.KJV: And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:
v.NASB: and James, the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James (to them He gave the name Boanerges, which means, 'Sons of Thunder ');
v.NIV: James son of Zebedee and his brother John (to them he gave the name Boanerges, which means “sons of thunder”),
v.NRSV: James son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder);
v.SAENEW: '천둥의 아들'을 뜻하는 보아너게라는 이름을 덧붙여 주신 세베대의 아들들인 야고보와, 그의 동생 요한과,
v.WLB: ‘우레의 아들들’이라는 뜻으로 ‘보아너게’라 이름 지어 준 세베대의 아들 야고보와 그 동생 요한,

v.CNV: 安得烈、腓力、巴多罗迈、马太、多马、亚勒腓的儿子雅各、达太、激进派的西门，
v.ESV: Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Zealot,
v.GAE: 또 안드레와 빌립과 바돌로매와 마태와 도마와 알패오의 아들 야고보와 및 다대오와 가나나인 시몬이며
v.KJV: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Canaanite,
v.NASB: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Zealot;
v.NIV: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot
v.NRSV: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean,
v.SAENEW: 안드레와 빌립과 바돌로매와 마태와 도마와 알패오의 아들 야고보와 다대오와 열혈당원 시몬과,
v.WLB: 안드레, 빌립, 바돌로매, 마태, 도마, 알패오의 아들 야고보, 다대오, 열심당원 시몬과

v.CNV: 以及加略人犹大，就是出卖耶稣的那个人。
v.ESV: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
v.GAE: 또 가룟 유다니 이는 예수를 판 자더라
v.KJV: And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.
v.NASB: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Him.
v.NIV: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
v.NRSV: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him. Then he went home;
v.SAENEW: 예수를 넘겨준 가룟 유다이다.
v.WLB: 예수를 배반한 가룟 유다였습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣进了屋子，群众又聚了来，以致他们连饭都不能吃，
v.ESV: Then he went home, and the crowd gathered again, so that they could not even eat.
v.GAE: 집에 들어가시니 무리가 다시 모이므로 식사할 겨를도 없는지라
v.KJV: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
v.NASB: And He came home, and the crowd gathered again, to such an extent that they could not even eat a meal.
v.NIV: Then Jesus entered a house, and again a crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples were not even able to eat.
v.NRSV: and the crowd came together again, so that they could not even eat.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 집에 들어가시니, 무리가 다시 모여들어서, 예수의 일행은 음식을 먹을 겨를도 없었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 집으로 들어가시니 또다시 사람들이 몰려들어 예수와 제자들은 음식 먹을 겨를조차 없었습니다.

v.CNV: 那些和他在一起的人听见了，就出来抓住他，因为他们说他癫狂了。
v.ESV: And when his family heard it, they went out to seize him, for they were saying, “He is out of his mind.”
v.GAE: 예수의 친족들이 듣고 그를 붙들러 나오니 이는 그가 미쳤다 함일러라
v.KJV: And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.
v.NASB: When His own people heard of this, they went out to take custody of Him; for they were saying, 'He has lost His senses.'
v.NIV: When his family heard about this, they went to take charge of him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.”
v.NRSV: When his family heard it, they went out to restrain him, for people were saying, “He has gone out of his mind.”
v.SAENEW: 예수의 가족들이, 예수가 미쳤다는 소문을 듣고서, 그를 붙잡으러 나섰다.
v.WLB: 예수의 가족들은 “예수가 미쳤다”는 소문을 듣고서 예수를 붙잡으러 찾아다녔습니다.

v.CNV: 从耶路撒冷下来的经学家说：“他有别西卜附在身上！”又说：“他靠着鬼王赶鬼。”
v.ESV: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem were saying, “He is possessed by Beelzebul,” and “by the prince of demons he casts out the demons.”
v.GAE: 예루살렘에서 내려온 서기관들은 그가 바알세불이 지폈다 하며 또 귀신의 왕을 힘입어 귀신을 쫓아낸다 하니
v.KJV: And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.
v.NASB: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem were saying, 'He is possessed by Beelzebul,' and 'He casts out the demons by the ruler of the demons.'
v.NIV: And the teachers of the law who came down from Jerusalem said, “He is possessed by Beelzebul! By the prince of demons he is driving out demons.”
v.NRSV: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul, and by the ruler of the demons he casts out demons.”
v.SAENEW: 예루살렘에서 내려온 율법학자들은, 예수가 바알세불이 들렸다고 하고, 또 그가 귀신의 두목의 힘을 빌어서 귀신을 쫓아낸다고도 하였다.
v.WLB: 그래서 예루살렘에서 내려온 율법학자들이 말했습니다. “예수가 바알세불에게 사로잡혀 있다. 그가 귀신들의 우두머리의 힘을 빌려 귀신을 쫓아내는 것이다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣把他们叫来，用比喻对他们说：“撒但怎能赶逐撒但呢？
v.ESV: And he called them to him and said to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
v.GAE: 예수께서 그들을 불러다가 비유로 말씀하시되 사탄이 어찌 사탄을 쫓아낼 수 있느냐
v.KJV: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
v.NASB: And He called them to Himself and began speaking to them in parables, 'How can Satan cast out Satan?
v.NIV: So Jesus called them over to him and began to speak to them in parables: “How can Satan drive out Satan?
v.NRSV: And he called them to him, and spoke to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
v.SAENEW: 그래서 예수께서 그들을 불러 놓고, 비유로 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "사탄이 어떻게 사탄을 쫓아낼 수 있느냐?
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서 그들을 불러 놓고 비유로 말씀하셨습니다. “사탄이 어떻게 사탄을 쫓아낼 수 있느냐?

v.CNV: 一国若自相纷争，那国就站立不住；
v.ESV: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
v.GAE: 또 만일 나라가 스스로 분쟁하면 그 나라가 설 수 없고
v.KJV: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
v.NASB: 'If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
v.NIV: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
v.NRSV: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
v.SAENEW: 한 나라가 갈라져서 서로 싸우면, 그 나라는 버틸 수 없다.
v.WLB: 만일 한 나라가 서로 갈라져 싸우면 그 나라가 제대로 서 있을 수 없고

v.CNV: 一家若自相纷争，那家就站立不住。
v.ESV: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
v.GAE: 만일 집이 스스로 분쟁하면 그 집이 설 수 없고
v.KJV: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
v.NASB: 'If a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
v.NIV: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
v.NRSV: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
v.SAENEW: 또 한 가정이 갈라져서 싸우면, 그 가정은 버티지 못할 것이다.
v.WLB: 만일 한 가정이 서로 갈라져 싸우면 그 가정이 제대로 서 있을 수 없다.

v.CNV: 如果撒但自相攻打纷争，不但站立不住，而且还要灭亡。
v.ESV: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but is coming to an end.
v.GAE: 만일 사탄이 자기를 거슬러 일어나 분쟁하면 설 수 없고 망하느니라
v.KJV: And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
v.NASB: 'If Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but he is finished!
v.NIV: And if Satan opposes himself and is divided, he cannot stand; his end has come.
v.NRSV: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but his end has come.
v.SAENEW: 사탄이 스스로에게 반란을 일으켜서 갈라지면, 버틸 수 없고, 끝장이 난다.
v.WLB: 만일 사탄이 스스로 반란을 일으켜 갈라진다면 제대로 서지 못하고 스스로 망할 것이다.

v.CNV: 谁都不能进入壮汉的家，抢夺他的财物，除非先把壮汉捆绑起来，才可以抢劫他的家。
v.ESV: But no one can enter a strong man's house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man. Then indeed he may plunder his house.
v.GAE: 사람이 먼저 강한 자를 결박하지 않고는 그 강한 자의 집에 들어가 세간을 강탈하지 못하리니 결박한 후에야 그 집을 강탈하리라
v.KJV: No man can enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.
v.NASB: 'But no one can enter the strong man's house and plunder his property unless he first binds the strong man, and then he will plunder his house.
v.NIV: In fact, no one can enter a strong man’s house without first tying him up. Then he can plunder the strong man’s house.
v.NRSV: But no one can enter a strong man’s house and plunder his property without first tying up the strong man; then indeed the house can be plundered.
v.SAENEW: 먼저 힘센 사람을 묶어 놓지 않고서는, 아무도 그 사람의 집에 들어가서 세간을 털어 갈 수 없다. 묶어 놓은 뒤에야, 그 집을 털어 갈 것이다.
v.WLB: 먼저 힘센 사람을 묶어 놓지 않고 그 집에 들어가 물건을 훔치는 사람은 아무도 없다. 묶고 나서야 그 집을 털 수 있는 것이다.

v.CNV: 我实在告诉你们，世人的一切罪和一切亵渎的话，都可以得到赦免；
v.ESV: “Truly, I say to you, all sins will be forgiven the children of man, and whatever blasphemies they utter,
v.GAE: 내가 진실로 너희에게 이르노니 사람의 모든 죄와 모든 모독하는 일은 사하심을 얻되
v.KJV: Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
v.NASB: 'Truly I say to you, all sins shall be forgiven the sons of men, and whatever blasphemies they utter;
v.NIV: Truly I tell you, people can be forgiven all their sins and every slander they utter,
v.NRSV: “Truly I tell you, people will be forgiven for their sins and whatever blasphemies they utter;
v.SAENEW: 내가 진정으로 너희에게 말한다. 사람들이 짓는 모든 죄와 그들이 하는 어떤 비방도 용서를 받을 것이다.
v.WLB: 내가 너희에게 진실로 말한다. 사람이 어떤 죄를 짓든지 어떤 비방의 말을 하든지 그것은 모두 용서받을 수 있다.

v.CNV: 但亵渎圣灵的，就永世不得赦免，他还要担当罪恶到永远。”
v.ESV: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin” —
v.GAE: 누구든지 성령을 모독하는 자는 영원히 사하심을 얻지 못하고 영원한 죄가 되느니라 하시니
v.KJV: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:
v.NASB: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin '--
v.NIV: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven; they are guilty of an eternal sin.”
v.NRSV: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit can never have forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin” —
v.SAENEW: 그러나 성령을 모독하는 사람은 용서를 받지 못하고, 영원한 죄에 매인다."
v.WLB: 그러나 누구든지 성령을 모독하는 사람은 결코 용서받을 수 없다. 그것은 영원한 죄다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣说这话，是因为他们说他有污灵附在身上。
v.ESV: for they were saying, “He has an unclean spirit.”
v.GAE: 이는 그들이 말하기를 더러운 귀신이 들렸다 함이러라
v.KJV: Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
v.NASB: because they were saying, 'He has an unclean spirit.'
v.NIV: He said this because they were saying, “He has an impure spirit.”
v.NRSV: for they had said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 이 말씀을 하신 것은, 사람들이 "그는 악한 귀신이 들렸다" 하고 말하였기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 이 말씀을 하신 것은 사람들이 “그가 악한 귀신이 들렸다”라고 말했기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣的母亲和弟弟来了，站在外面，传话给他，叫他出来。
v.ESV: And his mother and his brothers came, and standing outside they sent to him and called him.
v.GAE: 그 때에 예수의 어머니와 동생들이 와서 밖에 서서 사람을 보내어 예수를 부르니
v.KJV: There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.
v.NASB: Then His mother and His brothers arrived, and standing outside they sent word to Him and called Him.
v.NIV: Then Jesus’ mother and brothers arrived. Standing outside, they sent someone in to call him.
v.NRSV: Then his mother and his brothers came; and standing outside, they sent to him and called him.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 예수의 어머니와 동생들이 찾아와, 바깥에 서서, 사람을 들여보내어 예수를 불렀다.
v.WLB: 그때 예수의 어머니와 형제들이 찾아왔습니다. 그들은 밖에 서서 사람을 시켜 예수를 불렀습니다.

v.CNV: 有许多人正围坐在耶稣身边，他们告诉他：“你看，你的母亲和弟弟（有些抄本在此有“妹妹”一词）在外面找你。”
v.ESV: And a crowd was sitting around him, and they said to him, “Your mother and your brothers are outside, seeking you.”
v.GAE: 무리가 예수를 둘러 앉았다가 여짜오되 보소서 당신의 어머니와 동생들과 누이들이 밖에서 찾나이다
v.KJV: And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
v.NASB: A crowd was sitting around Him, and they said to Him, 'Behold, Your mother and Your brothers are outside looking for You.'
v.NIV: A crowd was sitting around him, and they told him, “Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you.”
v.NRSV: A crowd was sitting around him; and they said to him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside, asking for you.”
v.SAENEW: 무리가 예수의 주위에 둘러앉아 있다가, 그에게 말하였다. "보십시오, 선생님의 어머니와 동생들과 누이들이 바깥에서 선생님을 찾고 있습니다."
v.WLB: 많은 사람들이 예수 곁에 둘러앉아 있었는데 그들이 예수께 말했습니다. “보십시오. 선생님의 어머니와 형제들이 밖에서 선생님을 찾고 계십니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣回答他们：“谁是我的母亲，我的弟兄呢？”
v.ESV: And he answered them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
v.GAE: 대답하시되 누가 내 어머니이며 동생들이냐 하시고
v.KJV: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
v.NASB: Answering them, He said, 'Who are My mother and My brothers?'
v.NIV: “Who are my mother and my brothers?” he asked.
v.NRSV: And he replied, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 대답하셨다. "누가 내 어머니이며, 내 형제들이냐?"
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들에게 물으셨습니다. “누가 내 어머니고 내 형제들이냐?”

v.CNV: 于是四面观看那些围坐的人，说：“你们看，我的母亲！我的弟兄！
v.ESV: And looking about at those who sat around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
v.GAE: 둘러 앉은 자들을 보시며 이르시되 내 어머니와 내 동생들을 보라
v.KJV: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
v.NASB: Looking about at those who were sitting around Him, He said, 'Behold My mother and My brothers!
v.NIV: Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
v.NRSV: And looking at those who sat around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
v.SAENEW: 그리고 주위에 둘러앉은 사람들을 둘러보시고 말씀하셨다. "보아라, 내 어머니와 내 형제자매들이다.
v.WLB: 그러고는 곁에 둘러앉은 그들을 보며 말씀하셨습니다. “보라. 내 어머니와 내 형제들이다.

v.CNV: 凡遵行　神旨意的，就是我的弟兄姊妹和母亲了。”
v.ESV: For whoever does the will of God, he is my brother and sister and mother.”
v.GAE: 누구든지 하나님의 뜻대로 행하는 자가 내 형제요 자매요 어머니이니라
v.KJV: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.
v.NASB: 'For whoever does the will of God, he is My brother and sister and mother.'
v.NIV: Whoever does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”
v.NRSV: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”
v.SAENEW: 누구든지 하나님의 뜻을 행하는 사람이 곧 내 형제요 자매요 어머니다."
v.WLB: 누구든지 하나님의 뜻을 행하는 사람이 바로 내 형제요, 자매요, 어머니다.”
